items.story_excerpt: Hi, HN, we’re building Mia & Leo: AI personalities backed by purpose-built duplex speech models for revenue calls. Teams building real-world agents told us that more than 40% of callers hang up within the first 30 seconds. On a revenue call, that meant a lost booking, an unpaid balance, a cold lead, or a customer who does not come back. This happens because
items.title: Show HN: Production duplex speech model for revenue calls

items.story_excerpt: I’ve been building Echo ( https://echo.tracerml.ai/ ), an experiment in making one AI system out of a pool of open-weight models rather than choosing a single model and using it for every task. It started with a simple experiment. I took a group of models, including GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.7 and others, and ran them on the same evaluations. Then I measured what wou
items.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
Stronger AI agents did more damage, not less
Sierra acquires TakeOff, the long-horizon AI agent platform
Accidental data loss in Claude Code and OpenAI Codex: when AI deletes user files
Fail-closed enforcement for AI agents: deny what you cannot verify

items.story_excerpt: hey hn, I wanted to share a side project we’ve been using and iterating at Hedgy for the past couple weeks. It’s our take on a self-hosted company brain that is powered entirely by our claude subscriptions. It includes a ClickHouse data lake for ingesting data and light knowledge infrastructure for storing knowledge about the data (e.g. this is how we count
items.title: Show HN: Setoku – Self-hosted knowledge server for AI agents

items.story_excerpt: I built this on a day off - didn't want to get out of bed, and the laptop with all my agents was across the room. So I made them come to me. fleet runs a fleet of AI coding agents (Claude Code or Codex) from one Telegram supergroup - each agent in its own topic. Voice or text, code/diffs/files come back to the chat. Headless (fires per message) or a live CLI
items.title: Show HN: Fleet – drive a fleet of Claude Code/Codex agents from Telegram
Show HN: Cloud for your AI Agent's tools/fuctions

items.story_excerpt: Hi Hacker News, I'm Louis. I built Screenpipe ( https://screenpipe.com ), an app that records your screen and audio locally (only!), and gives AI agents a searchable memory of what you've seen, said, and heard. This makes it easier to automate your repetitive tasks, turn them into SOPs (Standard Operating Procedure) and so on. I made a HN-style demo video at
items.title: Launch HN: Screenpipe (YC S26) – Record how you work and turn that into agents
A value-poisoning benchmark for consequential agent actions

items.story_excerpt: hey HN, Jonathan and Guy here, creators of OneCLI ( https://onecli.sh/ ). OneCLI is an open source vault for AI Agents. Traditional vaults are used to store your secrets and, on demand, provide them to you all in a secure way, trusting the person to keep them safe. We figured that in the agent's world, this is not the case, as you don't know what happens wit
items.title: Show HN: OneCLI – OSS credential gateway that keeps secrets out of AI agents

items.story_excerpt: Hi HN, we are Marcos and Harrison, cofounders of Palmier ( https://palmier.io ). We are building Palmier Pro, an open source macOS video editor, with built-in AI generation and a local MCP server that connects to your agent. Here are a few demos: - Making some AI transitions: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbM_-eR1GX4 - Multicam editing with Codex: https://
items.title: Show HN: Palmier Pro – Open-source macOS video editor built for AI
One ChatGPT link could smuggle a rogue AI agent into your company

items.story_excerpt: The current state of in-market autonomous AI is “want something done; trust that they do it”. The operation running the models hold all of the logs, and 1st party logs are inherently not the strongest. It’s: “I did my homework, I graded my homework.. my homework got a passing grade. Trust”. Cinchor is the lightweight tool that operations can use to share ver
items.title: Show HN: Verify what an AI agent did, then tamper with the record (no signup)
The challenges of building a reliable PR review agent
Sunglasses: An open-source input scanner for AI agents

From knowledge creation to application, PileaX helps you build a unified knowledge base and continuously optimizes the AI interaction expe
items.title: Show HN: Note taking and book Library, all-in-one AI KnowledgeBase

items.story_excerpt: Turned the archived USB-Uncensored-LLM into a full autonomous agent. 13 tools, 5 GGUF models, zero install. Works on Windows, Linux, Tails. Everything offline after setup.
items.title: Show HN: USB AI Agent – portable uncensored AI with 13 tools, runs from USB

items.story_excerpt: Hi HN, for about a year now I've been experimenting with AI agents and building my own home ecosystem; from the start I set out with the idea of an agent that behaves like a member of the family, not as a personal agent, and this made me clash very early first with OpenClaw's builtin memory, then I tested dozens of memory plugins without ever finding one tha
items.title: Show HN: Mwe-MCP – self-hosted memory for AI agents that knows who may know what
